# AI-Generated Optimization Tips:
### Cursor IDE Rules for Efficient Google Lyria 3 Pro Preview Usage

To optimize the use of Google Lyria 3 Pro Preview for coding while minimizing token usage and cost, consider the following five practical Cursor IDE rules. These rules incorporate prompt engineering tips to ensure efficient and cost-effective utilization.

#### Rule 1: Specify the Programming Language
```yml
# Rule to specify the programming language for code completion
name: Specify Language
pattern: |
  (write|create|generate) (code|program|script) in (language|using) (?P<language>[a-zA-Z]+)
action: |
  # Use the cheapest model for code completion, e.g., Lyria 3 Pro Preview
  # Specify the language to minimize ambiguity and token usage
  Write {language} code for {context}.
```

#### Rule 2: Provide Context and Constraints
```yml
# Rule to provide context and constraints for code generation
name: Provide Context
pattern: |
  (write|create|generate) (code|program|script) to (?P<action>[a-zA-Z]+) (?P<object>[a-zA-Z]+)
action: |
  # Provide context and constraints to reduce token usage and improve accuracy
  Write {language} code to {action} {object} with the following constraints: {constraints}.
```

#### Rule 3: Use Natural Language for Simple Queries
```yml
# Rule to use natural language for simple queries
name: Simple Query
pattern: |
  (what|how|when) (?P<query>[a-zA-Z]+)
action: |
  # Use natural language for simple queries to minimize token usage
  {query}.
```

#### Rule 4: Define Functions and Variables Clearly

pattern: |
